ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for a combined cucumber-lemon-green-tea infused drink. FOODON_03311737 (green tea beverage) is the closest parent category covering the dominant botanical ingredient.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL (Tea, green) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for a cucumber-lemon-green-tea blended cold beverage.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34728003 (Green tea substance) is the closest parent. No SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific blended beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 ml (300 g), equivalent to one glass or cup as typically served at a Singaporean hot pot restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component ingredients: Cucumber (raw), Lemon juice, Green tea (brewed) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries 11206, 09152, 14355
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific branded beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted blending of USDA SR Legacy data for brewed green tea (dominant base, ~85% by volume), cucumber (sliced, ~10%), and lemon juice (~5%). Per-100g values reflect the dilute aqueous nature of this cold infused beverage. Energy and macros are very low as expected for a lightly infused tea-based drink.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of green tea and low-carbohydrate infused beverages — analogous estimate based on brewed green tea and plain water GI reference values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific cucumber-lemon-green-tea beverage. Brewed green tea has a GI of approximately 0–1 per University of Sydney GI Database conventions for non-caloric or near-zero-calorie beverages. Glycemic index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive value) as this beverage contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates (~0.6 g per 100 g, ~1.8 g per 300 ml serving). Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 (GL = GI/100 × carbohydrate_g_per_serving = 1/100 × 1.8 ≈ 0.02, rounded to 0.0). gi_category is low per convention for essentially zero-GI beverages.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~2 mg per 100 g; ~6 mg per 300 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: This beverage is suitable for individuals with hypertension as it contains negligible sodium (~6 mg per serving), and green tea polyphenols have been associated with modest blood pressure reduction in observational studies; however, patients on antihypertensive medications should note that green tea may interact with certain drugs and should consult their physician.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and sugar content with a glycemic load of effectively zero per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contributes fewer than 2 g of carbohydrate per 300 ml serving and has a glycemic load of effectively zero; green tea catechins (EGCG) have been associated with improved insulin sensitivity in clinical studies, though unsweetened consumption is essential to maintain glycaemic benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of green tea catechins with cardioprotective associations
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage is considered cardiovascular-friendly due to its absence of saturated fat and cholesterol, and the green tea component provides catechins (particularly EGCG) that have been associated with reduced LDL oxidation and improved endothelial function in peer-reviewed cardiovascular research.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 'I'm Not A Fool' (我不是傻瓜, Wǒ bù shì shǎguā) is a playfully named cold infused beverage served at Hai Kah Lang, a Chinese hot pot restaurant at Funan Mall in Singapore. The drink is composed of three primary ingredients: fresh cucumber (黄瓜), lemon (柠檬), and green tea (绿茶), combined to create a light, refreshing, mildly astringent beverage with subtle vegetal sweetness from the cucumber, bright citrus acidity from the lemon, and the characteristic grassy-floral notes of green tea. It is served chilled and is typical of the modern Singaporean trend of offering house-crafted botanical beverages alongside hot pot meals. Nutritionally, this is an extremely low-calorie drink (~10 kcal per 300 ml serving) with negligible macronutrients, making it suitable for calorie-conscious diners, individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, and those with hypertension or cardiovascular concerns. The green tea base contributes polyphenols including epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), which has been studied for antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective properties. The cucumber contributes trace amounts of vitamin C, potassium, and cucurbitacins, while lemon juice adds a small amount of vitamin C and citric acid. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal-compatible. It is not sweetened in its base form as described, though restaurant preparation may vary. The whimsical Chinese name translates literally as 'I am not a fool', reflecting the playful branding culture of contemporary Singaporean dining establishments.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific branded beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component blending of USDA SR Legacy data for brewed green tea, raw cucumber, and lemon juice. The food identity, ingredient composition, and restaurant provenance are confirmed from the GrabFood Singapore source record. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component ingredients are well-characterised in approved databases and the blended estimate is defensible for a dilute aqueous beverage of this type.
notes: 1. fat_g is naturally zero for this dilute aqueous tea-based beverage; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for brewed green tea (FDC ID 14355) and cucumber (FDC ID 11206). 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all naturally zero for this plant-based infused beverage with no added oils or animal products. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is naturally zero as green tea, cucumber, and lemon contain no vitamin D. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy database for brewed green tea, and its contribution from cucumber and lemon at these dilution levels is negligible and not reliably estimable. 6. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA SR Legacy database for brewed green tea, and their estimation from minor component fractions (cucumber, lemon) at these dilution levels would introduce unacceptable uncertainty. 7. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates in meaningful quantity; gi_category is low per convention for essentially zero-GI beverages. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0. 8. Serving size of 300 g is an estimate based on standard beverage serving conventions at Singaporean restaurants; actual restaurant portion may vary. 9. The food name 'I'm Not A Fool' is a branded/menu name; the canonical English descriptor used in food_name reflects the actual composition. 10. Price SGD 7.09 is preserved from the GrabFood Singapore source record for reference only and is not used in nutritional calculations.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. Identified that fat_g=0.0 required zero-macro justification in notes. Added explicit 'naturally zero' justification phrases for fat_g, saturated_fat_g, and related zero values.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency — confirmed all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases list. Verified glycemic_index set to 1 (not null or 0) per zero-calorie beverage rule. Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified ontology match_type values are all from permitted set. Confirmed PASS on all hard gates and Q1-Q8.

Iteration 3: Addressed g3_numeric_hygiene REJECT from validator. Corrected `monounsaturated_fat_g`, `polyunsaturated_fat_g`, and `trans_fat_g` from -1.0 to 0.0 to align with 'naturally zero' justification. Revised phrasing for all remaining -1.0 values in `notes` (vitamin_a_mcg_rae, B vitamins, folate, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium) to explicitly state 'not reported in the USDA SR Legacy database' for clearer documentation of missing data basis.
